content_preview.title: FDA Grants Accelerated Approval to First Gene Therapy for Severe Leukocyte Adhesion Deficiency, Capping a Two-Year Regulatory Journey
content_preview.summary: Rocket Pharmaceuticals' Kresladi becomes the first gene therapy approved for LAD-I, an ultra-rare immune disorder that leaves children vulnerable to fatal infections.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration granted accelerated approval on March 27, 2026, to Kresladi (marnetegragene autotemcel), an autologous hematopoietic stem cell-based gene therapy for pediatric patients with severe leukocyte adhesion deficiency type I (LAD-I), according to the [FDA](https://www.fda.gov/news-events/press-announcements/fda-approves-first-gene-therapy-severe-leukocyte-adhesion-deficiency-type-i). It is the first therapy of any kind approved specifically for this ul...
